task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the specification brief.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer open showing the contents of 'arcticflow_chiller_spec.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent created physical geometry for the chiller in Blender.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender 3D viewport displays a newly created 3D model (e.g., a box or more complex shape) representing the chiller.

task_completion.description: Verify the object is classified as an MEP chiller.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlenderBIM/Bonsai Object Properties panel shows the IFC Class assigned as 'IfcChiller' or 'IfcEnergyConversionDevice'.

task_completion.description: Verify the manufacturer property set is added with the correct value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IFC Object Properties panel shows 'Pset_ManufacturerTypeInformation' assigned to the object, with the Manufacturer property explicitly set to 'ArcticFlow'.

task_completion.description: Verify at least two nested logical MEP connection ports are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Outliner or IFC Ports panel shows at least two 'IfcDistributionPort' (or 'IfcPort') elements nested under or associated with the main chiller object.

task_completion.description: Verify the final model is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Blender file save dialog or a terminal/file manager window showing 'arcticflow_chiller.ifc' being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the BlenderBIM/Bonsai graphical interface to author the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Blender UI (viewport, outliner, properties panels) rather than just writing a Python script to generate the IFC file via terminal.

integrity.description: Verify properties and ports were actually assigned within the BIM authoring tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Property sets and port classifications should be visible in the BlenderBIM interface panels, not just typed into a text file or mocked in a separate document.

integrity.description: Verify the agent authored the model from scratch as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots should show the progression of creating geometry and assigning IFC data, rather than simply downloading or copying a pre-existing complete IFC file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an IFC model saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/arcticflow_chiller.ifc'. The model must be classified as 'IfcChiller' or 'IfcEnergyConversionDevice'. It must contain 'Pset_ManufacturerTypeInformation' with the manufacturer set to 'ArcticFlow'. It must also include at least two ports classified as 'IfcDistributionPort' or 'IfcPort'. The specification document is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/arcticflow_chiller_spec.txt'.
